question: [File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getExchangeRate()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly call the exchange rate endpoint with crafted filterQueries that force fallbackRetry() to exhaust all 10 attempts and return null, causing the network to serve no valid exchange rate and effectively freezing fee-dependent transaction processing for the duration of the outage?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fallbackRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a filterQueries.whereQuery that pins the consensus_timestamp upper bound to a value where all 10 fallback iterations find only corrupt or unparseable file_data rows, causing fallbackRetry() to return null and stalling exchange-rate-dependent block validation for 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getFileData()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a far-past timestamp value to getFileData(fileId, timestamp) such that the getFileDataQuery subquery finds no anchor create/update row, returns NULL for the entire aggregation, and causes downstream block-processing logic to stall waiting for a valid file state that never arrives?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getLatestFileContentsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted innerWhere string through getLatestFileContentsQuery() that causes the filterOuterPlaceholder replacement via replaceAll('and ', 'and f.') to produce a syntactically valid but semantically wrong SQL clause, returning a stale consensus_timestamp that makes the mirror node serve an outdated exchange rate and delay block finalization beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getLatestFileDataContents()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a whereQuery array with a consensus_timestamp upper-bound filter set to a timestamp far in the past, causing getLatestFileDataContents() to return a file state from before the last 24-hour window and making the network operate on a stale exchange rate that causes transactions to be rejected as if the block were frozen?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fallbackRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the fallbackRetry() loop by ensuring the database contains 10 consecutive file_data rows with malformed protobuf payloads for the exchange rate file, causing each iteration to throw FileDecodeError, exhaust all attempts, and return null, which freezes transaction fee resolution for the duration of the block?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getFileData()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a timestamp of 0 or a negative value to getFileData(fileId, timestamp), causing the inner subquery in getFileDataQuery to find no matching create/update anchor and return NULL data, which propagates as a missing file state and delays block processing beyond the 500% threshold?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getLatestFileContentsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an innerWhere string containing multiple 'and ' substrings such that the replaceAll('and ', 'and f.') transformation in getLatestFileContentsQuery() produces a malformed SQL query that causes a database error, making the exchange rate endpoint unavailable and blocking block-time-sensitive transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fallbackRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause fallbackRetry() to perform 10 sequential database queries by ensuring each getLatestFileDataContents() call returns a row with a different first_consensus_timestamp, creating a chain of 10 DB round-trips that cumulatively delay the exchange rate response by 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getExchangeRate()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the getExchangeRate() endpoint with concurrent requests each carrying a whereQuery that forces fallbackRetry() to run all 10 iterations, saturating the database connection pool and causing block-processing queries to queue up, effectively delaying block finalization beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getFileData()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a maximum integer timestamp (e.g., Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER) to getFileData(fileId, timestamp), causing the getFileDataQuery to scan the entire file_data table for the entity, producing a full-table sequential scan that delays the response beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getLatestFileDataContents()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a whereQuery with an array-valued param (exploiting the Array.isArray branch in buildWhereSqlStatement) containing thousands of timestamp values, generating an IN clause that causes a slow database scan and delays the exchange rate response beyond the block time threshold?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fallbackRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that fallbackRetry() accumulates filters by spreading whereQuery on each iteration (filters.whereQuery = [...whereQuery, {query, param}]) to craft an initial whereQuery that, after 10 iterations, produces a SQL WHERE clause so large it causes a database timeout, delaying block-time-sensitive exchange rate resolution?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getLatestFileContentsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an innerWhere value that, after the replaceAll transformation, causes the outer WHERE clause to reference a non-existent table alias, producing a SQL error that makes getLatestFileDataContents() return null and causes the exchange rate to be unavailable during block processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getFileData()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a fileId corresponding to the exchange rate system file and a timestamp just before a known file update, causing getFileData() to return a stale exchange rate that makes all transactions in the current block fail fee validation, effectively freezing the block?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fallbackRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where fallbackRetry() returns null after 10 attempts because the whereQuery timestamp filter excludes all valid file_data rows, causing the ExchangeRate constructor to never be called and leaving the network without a valid exchange rate for the duration of block processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getLatestFileContentsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft an innerWhere string that, when processed by replaceAll('and ', 'and f.'), produces a tautological condition (e.g., 1=1) that bypasses the transaction_type filter in latestFileContentsQuery, returning file_data from non-create/update transactions and causing the exchange rate to be computed from corrupted concatenated data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getExchangeRate()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a whereQuery that sets consensus_timestamp to a value in the future (beyond the current block), causing getExchangeRate() to return null because no file_data rows exist at that timestamp, and making the network unable to price transactions for the current block?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getFileData()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly call getFileData() with different fileId values that do not exist in the file_data table, causing getSingleRow() to return null each time and forcing any dependent block-processing logic to retry indefinitely, delaying block finalization beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fallbackRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that fallbackRetry() uses row.first_consensus_timestamp (the MIN of consensus_timestamp in the aggregation) as the upper bound for the next iteration, and craft a scenario where first_consensus_timestamp equals consensus_timestamp, causing the loop to query the same time window repeatedly and never converge, wasting 10 DB round-trips?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getLatestFileDataContents()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a whereQuery with a consensus_timestamp lower-bound filter that excludes the most recent create/update transaction (transaction_type 17 or 19), causing the latest_create CTE in latestFileContentsQuery to return no rows and the outer query to return null, making the exchange rate unavailable for block processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getLatestFileContentsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an innerWhere string that contains the literal text '<filterInnerPlaceHolder>' or '<filterOuterPlaceHolder>', causing the .replace() calls in getLatestFileContentsQuery() to produce a doubly-substituted or malformed SQL query that errors out and makes the exchange rate endpoint return null?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getFileData()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a fileId of the exchange rate file (EntityId.systemEntity.exchangeRateFile) and a timestamp that falls between two consecutive file updates, causing getFileData() to return a partial or concatenated file_data blob that fails protobuf parsing and makes the exchange rate unavailable for the current block?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fallbackRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause fallbackRetry() to log 10 warning messages via logger.warn() with large error strings, creating a log-flooding side effect that degrades the mirror node's I/O performance and indirectly delays block-processing queries beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getLatestFileDataContents()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a whereQuery array with a very large number of filter conditions, causing buildWhereSqlStatement() to generate a SQL WHERE clause with hundreds of AND conditions that forces a full sequential scan of the file_data table and delays the exchange rate response beyond the block time threshold?
